methodsAttempted.query: site:du.edu University of Denver AI policy artificial intelligence students faculty generative AI academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located official University of Denver Office of Teaching and Learning guidance, an Academic Affairs University Writing Program statement, a chancellor news/reflection item, a faculty feature PDF, and an official SharePoint PDF link.
robots.txt allows the Office of Teaching and Learning page path; HTTP HEAD returned 200 for the AI classroom concerns page.
robots.txt allows the Academic Affairs writing statement path; HTTP HEAD returned 200 for the generative AI and writing statement.
robots.txt allows the news path; the chancellor reflection was official but not used for policy claims because it is a broad news/reflection item rather than guidance.

The official SharePoint PDF link from the Academic Affairs page redirected to Microsoft sign-in in Firecrawl and returned 403 access denied through direct HTTP, so it was recorded as login-gated/inaccessible.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found two usable University of Denver sources: an Office of Teaching and Learning page on AI concerns in the classroom and an Academic Affairs University Writing Program statement on generative AI and writing. The chancellor news/reflection item was rejected for low policy specificity, and the linked SharePoint PDF was rejected as inaccessible because it required Microsoft sign-in or returned 403.

targets.sourceTitle: Concerns About Student Use - Office of Teaching & Learning
Statement on Generative AI and Writing

stopConditions: Stop after official University of Denver guidance supports narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer binding university-wide policy from news/reflection, faculty feature, or login-gated sources.
Keep all claim review states agent_reviewed and do not set canonical publication flags.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Denver Office of Teaching and Learning advises faculty to state clear expectations for student work and to consider syllabus statements explaining whether and how AI tools can be used.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Faculty-facing AI classroom guidance recommends clear course-level AI expectations and syllabus language.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Concerns About Student Use - Office of Teaching & Learning
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Don't rely on AI checking software to confirm your suspicions. Although Turnitin and other companies have programs that check for AI-generated writing, many of these programs are, at best, in their earliest stages, or at worse, unreliable.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Statement on Generative AI and Writing
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Before using any such technology, review the policies and constraints of the rhetorical situation. As a student, this means abiding by instructor policies for assignments communicated in the syllabus, assignment, or verbally.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Responsible and Ethical Use of GenAI; For writers

artifacts.sourceTitle: Statement on Generative AI and Writing
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Review the End-User-License-Agreements (EULAs), privacy policies, and other use documentation so that you understand your rights, the rights of the developer, and the use of data you provide to a company. Never input private, propriety, or protected data to a third-party website without understanding these policies.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Statement on Generative AI and Writing
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Acknowledge that we are aware that genAI exists and outline clear policies about when and how it can or cannot be used in an assignment or task. Blanket syllabus statements forbidding or giving carte blanche are not as helpful as outlining the ways genAI might be used for specific tasks or giving specific directions not to use genAI for certain parts of the writing process.

artifacts.title: University of Denver AI Policy Evidence Update
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for University of Denver is limited to official guidance from the Office of Teaching and Learning and the University Writing Program. Claims cover faculty syllabus clarity, AI detector caution, student compliance with instructor policies, third-party data warnings, and writing-assignment genAI policy guidance.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The SharePoint PDF linked by Academic Affairs was inaccessible without Microsoft sign-in, so no claims rely on it.
The official chancellor reflection/news page was not used as policy evidence because it is broad commentary rather than operational guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
